summary.note: The changed-files set contains no eligible commands/*.md or agents/*.md files. findings=[]; files_inspected=0. Any inline source comments and docstrings in the six hook/test files are implementation context in executable files and are outside prompt-inspector markdown-section detection. No target markdown section headers (## Philosophy, ## Overview, ## Quality Standards, ## Safety Features, ## Helper Scripts, ## Usage) were detected in any of the listed files.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed the zero-violation conclusion is correct. Two actionable issues with proposed fixes were returned: (1) the draft misstated scope by treating .py/.sh files as scored targets — they should be excluded_files with files_inspected=0; (2) the characterization 'load-bearing comments and docstrings' was replaced with a precise scope-based exclusion reason. One observation flagged: no false positives, missed verbosity, or severity misclassification found.

findings.issue: Draft 'verbose_lines = 0 for all files' slightly misstated scope by treating .py/.sh hook/test files as inspected prompt-doc targets. Prompt-inspector scope is command/agent markdown docs, so these files should be excluded, not scored.
findings.proposed_fix: The changed-files set contains no eligible commands/*.md or agents/*.md files. findings=[]; files_inspected=0; list the six hook/test files under excluded_files with reason 'outside prompt-inspector markdown scope'.
findings.rationale: Codex is correct: counting .py/.sh files as inspected targets misrepresents the inspector's scope contract. Applied: files_inspected set to 0, all six files moved to excluded_files block with scope reason.

findings.issue: 'All 6 changed files contain only load-bearing comments and docstrings' is broader and more subjective than needed. Some source comments are long, but source comments are outside this inspector's markdown-section rules.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace with: 'Any inline source comments/docstrings are implementation context in executable hook/test files and are outside prompt-inspector markdown-section detection. No target markdown headers such as ## Philosophy, ## Overview, ## Quality Standards, ## Safety Features, ## Helper Scripts, or ## Usage were detected.'
findings.rationale: Applied as proposed. The replacement wording is precise and grounded in the inspector's detection rules rather than a subjective judgment about comment quality.

findings.issue: No false positives, missed critical prompt-doc verbosity, or severity misclassification found. The listed files are non-markdown source/test files, and severity thresholds apply to verbose_lines, not total file line counts.
findings.rationale: Confirms the analysis is correct. No action required.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied both blocker fixes: (1) files_inspected set to 0, all six files in excluded_files block; (2) summary wording replaced with scope-precise exclusion reason referencing the markdown-section detection rules.
